mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns are negative in >55% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand growth that drives corporate revenue expectations higher, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative forward returns in >50% of cases or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp month-over-month contractions in the industrial production index reflect immediate supply-side bottlenecks that compress profit margins and cause negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >65% of contraction months or the signal fails to predict direction during periods of stable capacity utilization.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation combined with a stable or rising federal funds rate indicates monetary tightening that increases discount rates and reduces equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL increases > 0.3% month-over-month while FEDFUNDS does not decline
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >55% of triggered months or the interaction effect reverses sign during disinflationary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate income and consumer spending capacity, which supports corporate earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >50% of positive payroll months or the correlation between payroll growth and equity returns drops below 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly real GDP growth rate falling below 1.5% signals weakening aggregate demand that reduces corporate revenue forecasts and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of low-growth quarters or the threshold effect disappears when controlling for monetary policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling unemployment indicates labor market tightness that raises wage pressures and compresses margins, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >55% of divergence instances or the mechanism fails during periods of productivity-driven output growth.
